verses.text: David subdues the Philistines and Moab, who become his servants. The promise that his enemies would be subdued begins to come true. What God pledged in covenant He performs in history, victory by victory.

David subdues the Philistines and Moab, who become his servants. The promise that his enemies would be subdued begins to come true. What God pledged in covenant He performs in history, victory by victory.

David defeats Hadarezer and the Syrians, garrisons their land, and 'the LORD preserved David whithersoever he went.' The refrain names the secret of his success. His triumphs were not his own strength but God's keeping hand.

David defeats Hadarezer and the Syrians, garrisons their land, and 'the LORD preserved David whithersoever he went.' The refrain names the secret of his success. His triumphs were not his own strength but God's keeping hand.

David defeats Hadarezer and the Syrians, garrisons their land, and 'the LORD preserved David whithersoever he went.' The refrain names the secret of his success. His triumphs were not his own strength but God's keeping hand.

David defeats Hadarezer and the Syrians, garrisons their land, and 'the LORD preserved David whithersoever he went.' The refrain names the secret of his success. His triumphs were not his own strength but God's keeping hand.

David carries off gold shields and much brass — the very brass Solomon would later use for the temple. The spoils of war are gathered for God's house. God provisions His future work even through present battles.

David carries off gold shields and much brass — the very brass Solomon would later use for the temple. The spoils of war are gathered for God's house. God provisions His future work even through present battles.

When Tou sends gifts, David dedicates all the silver and gold, from every nation, to the LORD. He does not hoard his spoils but consecrates them. The truest use of what victory brings is to lay it at God's feet.

When Tou sends gifts, David dedicates all the silver and gold, from every nation, to the LORD. He does not hoard his spoils but consecrates them. The truest use of what victory brings is to lay it at God's feet.

When Tou sends gifts, David dedicates all the silver and gold, from every nation, to the LORD. He does not hoard his spoils but consecrates them. The truest use of what victory brings is to lay it at God's feet.
Edom is subdued and garrisoned, and again 'the LORD preserved David whithersoever he went.' The refrain sounds twice, lest we miss it. Every triumph, everywhere, is traced to the LORD who kept him.
Edom is subdued and garrisoned, and again 'the LORD preserved David whithersoever he went.' The refrain sounds twice, lest we miss it. Every triumph, everywhere, is traced to the LORD who kept him.

David reigns over all Israel, executing 'judgment and justice unto all his people,' with capable officers in place. The conqueror abroad is a just ruler at home. True greatness is measured by righteousness done, not only battles won.
